steps.title: Identify what explaining to a new audience requires
steps.content:
The question asks which choice best explains the 50 Completers challenge to someone unfamiliar with it.

A new audience needs a complete definition: what the challenge is, what the exact requirement is, which group/club created it, and what achieving it means.

Not an anecdote about one person, not a partial description, not an invitation.

steps.title: Match this requirement to the choices
steps.content:
Only B provides all components: it states the requirement clearly (reaching the highest points in all fifty US states), names the club (the Highpointers Club), defines the achievement term (50 Completers), and illustrates the scope with specific examples introduced by including to show they're examples of the broader requirement, not the requirement itself.

The other three lack essential components—
  • A distorts the requirement by presenting two examples as the full challenge
  • C omits the specific examples and never explains what 50 Completers means
  • D tells one person's story rather than defining the challenge
.

That is the correct answer.
